def: s.m. A friend, one beloved; a lover, a sweetheart
more: n. m. 1. A friend (H. mitr ). 1. Koī ilm ko dost rakhtā hai, koī rupaë ko . Prov. Some learning, and some money prize. 2. Chiṛyā ne dekh kīṛā gāfil idhar ghasīṭā, Kavve ne waqt pā-kar chiṛyā kā par ghasīṭā, Chīloṅ ne mār panjë kauvve kā sar ghasīṭā, Jo jis ke hāth āyā us ne hī dhar ghasīṭā, Hushyār, yār jānī! yeh dasht hai ṭhagoṅ kā, Yāṅ ṭuk nigāh chūkī, aur māl dostoṅ kā . Nazīr. The sparrow sees his chance and drags the worm away, The crow just bides his time and bears the bird away, The kite falls on the crow and snaps his head away: Beware, my friend! a world of cut-throats this all keen to slay, Your eye once off, your goods these friends to their own maws convey. 3. Dostoṅ kā hisāb dil meṅ . Prov. Friends understand one another. 2. A lover; sweetheart; paramour. dost-dār, dos-dār , adj. Friendly. dost-dārī , n. f. Friendliness. dost rakhnā , v. a. wety: Inherited from Old Hindi दो (do), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑁄 (do), from Sanskrit द्व (dvá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dwáH, from Proto-Indo-European *dwóh₁.

def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (to God); an invocation of good, a blessing, benediction; wish; congratulation, salutation; imprecation of evil, curse, maiediction ( = bad-duʻā); — a verse, or verses, of the Qorʼān; — an incantation (pl. adʻiyat, or vulg. adʻiya)
more: n. دعو prayed. 1. Benediction; blessing. 2. Prayer; invocation; an incantation. 3. An āyat or text from the Qurān. Duā aur davā donoṅ karnī chāhiye . Prov. Prayer and medicine should go together. duā paṛh'nā , v. a. To read the praises of God in the Korān. Kaī ham-dameṅ thīṅ jo kuchh kuchh paṛhīṅ, Duāëṅ wo paṛh-paṛh-ke āge baṛhīṅ . Mīr Hasan. duā-i-khair , n. f. A prayer for one's welfare. duā-i-daulat , Prayer for one's prosperity; benediction. duā denā , v. a. To give a blessing; to bless or pray for. duā salām kahnā , v. a. To send one's compliments or blessings. duā-go , n. m. 1. One who blesses or prays; beadsman. 2. A well-wisher; a friend. duā māṅgnā, dast ba-duā honā , v. n. To pray for; bless; to lift up (one's hands in prayer). Māṅgā kareṅge ab se duā hijr-i-yār kī, Akhir to dushmanī hai asar ko duā ke sāth . Momin. bad-duā ; H. sarāp , n. m. Calling down curses on. Kisī kī bad…

s. f. Benediction, blessing, salutation, congratulation, prayer, wish, invocation, imprecation. (Plur. ادعيه). دعاي دولت duʼāﻌ-e-daulat, Praying for oneʼs prosperity, benediction. دعا دينا duʼāﻌ denā, To give a blessing, to bless or pray for. دعا کرني يا مانگني duʼāﻌ-karnī or -māngnī, To wish for, to pray. دعا گو duʼāﻌ-go, One who blesses or prays, blesser. دعا گوي duʼāﻌ-go-ī, s. f. Benediction, blessing.
subs: s.f. Prayer for the welfare (of anyone)
s.f. Prayer for the prosperity (of), benediction
(-ko), To give a blessing (to), to bless, to pray for
s.m. One who invokes a blessing, one who blesses or prays, a blesser; a well-wisher, friend
s.f. Invocation of a blessing, benediction, blessing
ex: It's your prayer (it's thanks to you); I'm fine, thanks
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُعَا (du'ā). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi دعا (d'a /⁠du'ā⁠/).

more: n. f. S. दूर्बा a grass, Pālī, dubbā . A fine soft green grass, highly esteemed for fodder; Cynodor dactylon . Wat. 1. Zamīdārī dūb kī jaṛ hai . Prov. An estate, like the roots of the dūb , is always bearing. 2. Nānuk! nannhā ho raho jaisi nannhī dūb! Aur ghās jal jāeṅgī, dūb khūb kī khūb . Nānak Shāh. Be modest Nānak! as the fresh soft dūb doth lowly lie, While other grasses scorched up are, the dūb's bloom ne'er doth die. 3. Dahī, dūrbā, rochan, phal mūlā, Nav tulsī dal, maṅgal mūlā . Rāmāyan. dūb kī nāl, lit . the sheath of dūb ; hence, Family connections, so called from their tendency to spread far and wide like dūb. kheṛe kī dūb, lit . the grass of a common. A poor man of no account.
E. n. m. S. द्रुड to sink. Dip; immersion; soaking.

def: adv. Cor. of P. rū-ba-rū , S. द्वयोरुपरिद्दे Face to face; tête-a-tête ; before; in the presence of. Laṛūtī khasam se tumheṅ dū-ba-dū hai! Buā, kis tarah kī tumhārī bahū hai? Wom. Rāhat. She sets you and her husband fighting constantly, Sister, what sort of daughter-in-law should this one be? dū-ba-dū kahnā , v. a. To say before one's face. dū-ba-dū h. , v. n. 1. To come face to face; to be confronted with. 2. To fall out; wrangle; abuse one another. Āj to in donoṅ kī khūb dū-ba-dū huī .

s.f. Drowning, &c.; — an unconditional bribe (given whether the donor gain or lose his suit, in contradistinction to tirānī or tarāʼī, a bribe which is to be returned if the suit be lost).

n. f. 1. Drowning. 2. A bribe which is given absolutely, whether the briber gain or lose his suit, in contradistinction to Tirānī , a crossing over, i. e. a bribe which is to be returned if the suit is lost. ḍubvā'nā, ḍubānā , Caus. of دَبونا q. v. pasīnoṅ meṅ ḍūbnā , v. n. more: n. f. S. द्वैधक, Pālī, dvelhakam . 1. Doubt; suspense; dilemma. 2. Ambiguity; uncertainty. 1. Apne apne manan kī sabhoṅ ne līnī mān, Satt dharam dubdhā rahī, parī na kāhū jān . Tulsī Dās. All go their own ways just as each one different thinks, The true faith none hath found, each one misdoubting shrinks. 2. Musalmān uṭh Makke jāve, Hindū Gangā nhātā hai; Dubdhā hī meṅ janam gaṅvāyā, nis din gāfil rahtā hai . Brindsatsahī. The Musalman to Mecca goes, in the Ganges Hindus dip, In doubts and heedlessness their nights and days all from them slip. dubdhā k. , v. n. To be in a state of doubt; to doubt; mistrust ( āgā pīchhā k .).
s. f. Doubt, suspense, uncertainty, dilemma.
subs: v.n. To be in a state of doubt, to doubt, to mistrust.

more: adj. S. दूर्भर, Pālī. dūharo . See اجيرن 7, 9—11. 1. Chhuddar laṛikā se to jāno dūbhar ho jāle . Bhoj. A wicked child makes life a burden. 2. Ek bīr saë rakhnī dūbhar, Kaṭhan rākhnā saë dū kā! W. Rus. song. One wife to keep is hard for you, How hard 'twould be to keep wives two! 3. Tum mujhe dūbhar nahīṅ ho . You are not a burden to me. 4. Chain is bachche ke rone se nahīṅ dam bhar mujhe, Khojṛe-pīṭe ne jīnā kar dīyā dūbhar mujhe . Wom. Rāhat. This child is always crying, not a moment's peace have I, The wretch just makes me drag a life of longdrawn misery. 5. Arshad, aise ishq meṅ sadme sahe, Ishq bhī ab mujh ko dūbhar ho gayā . Arshad. Love's darts have wounded Arshad so That Love to him 's unwelcome now.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Lean, thin, meagre; poor, barren; weak, feeble.
more: adj. S. दुर्बल, from dur neg., bal strength. See patlā , 3. 1. Duble kalāṅvat kī kaun suntā hai? Prov. Who listens to a poor singer? (Who ever heeds the voice of the poor?). 2. ' Kyoṅ kyoṅ, re bhaīyā, dublā kyoṅ, aur kyoṅ terā mailā bhes?' 'Bāṭ chalat, jījī, dūblā, dhūp paṛat mailā bhes .' Suhāg. 'Why why, my brother, art so thin, why browned thy face? 'Travelling on foot hath made me thin, sunburnt my face!' dublā-pan, dublāpā , n. f. Leanness; emaciation.

ety: ḍūb˚, per met. = būḍ˚ = Prk. बुड्ड(इ) = S. व्रुड(ति), rt. व्रुड्
def: v.n. To dive; to sink, drown, be drowned; to drown oneself; to be immersed, be submerged, inundated, deluged, or flooded; to be lost, be sacrificed (as capital, reputation, &c.); to be destroyed, be ruined; to sink, go down, set (as the sun); to sink, faint (as the heart); to be absorbed, be engrossed, be lost (in business, or study, or thought, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To dive. 2. To drown. 3. To be immerged or deluged. 4. To set, to sink; to be destroyed or ruined. 5. To be absorbed (in business, study, &c.). دل ڐوبنا dil-ḍūbnā, To faint.
subs: v.n. To be drowned, die by drowning; to drown oneself.

def: v. n. 1. To drown; to be immersed, or deluged. 1. Tairegā so ḍūbegā . Prov. Who swims sinks. 2. Lāo kuāṅ! maiṅ ḍūbūṅ . Prov. Bring the well! I'll drown myself. 3. Kāgaz kī nāo āj na ḍūbī, kal ḍūbī . Prov. A paper boat, if not to-day, to-morrow sure will sink. 4. Ḍūbte ko tinke kā sahārā bahut . Prov. A drowning man will catch at a straw. 5. Woh din ḍubbe jo ghoṛī chaṛheṅ kubbe . Prov. The days are gone when humpbacks rode. (Said to the incompetent.) 6. Woh ḍūbeṅ maṅjhdhār jin par bhārī bojh . Prov. He sinks midstream who bears a heavy load of sins. 7. Āp ḍūbe bāmnā. jijmānoṅ le ḍūbe . Prov. When the Brahman sinks his constituents go down with him. 8. Ḍāl oṛhnā gal meṅ tere, donoṅ mūchh ukhāṛūṅ! Ḍūb marūṅ yā nikal jāūṅgī, ijjat teri bigāṛūṅ . Play. I'll drag you with my sheet around your neck and pluck out your moustache, I'll leave your house and drown myself, but foul disgrace shall you abash. 9. Kahīṅ ḍūbe bhī tire hain? Prov. What has sunk will never swim again. 2. To set; sink. Sūraj ḍūbnā . 3. To be engrossed, absorbed, buried, wrapt in. 4. To be ruined, lost, smashed, done for. Rupayā ḍūb gayā . 5. To be disgraced; to get a bad name. Nām ḍūbnā. ḍūbā-huā kharch . Expenditure without return ( baṭṭe khāte ). ḍūbā-huā khātā , An account or register of bad debts. ḍūb mar'nā , v. n. To drown oneself. ḍūban beṛā , n. m. lit . a sinking ship. An unnatural offence; a damning sin. ḍūban beṛā paṛnā , v. n. To be committed (a damning sin). ḍūbant yā burī ṭīp . A dead or bad bond. ḍūbant khātā , An irrecoverable balance.

more: (caus. of ڐوبنا) v. a. To drown, immerse, flood; to ruin, &c. see ڐبانا ḍubānā. ڐبودينا ḍubo denā, v. a. To exhaust, to ruin, to demolish, to drown.

def: v. a. 1. To drown; immerse; submerge; flood. 1. Nāo kinne ḍaboī ? Khuāja khizar ne . Prov. Who drowned the boat? Khwaja Khizar. 2. Bhūl gaë sab gyān shāstar paṛh-kar sabhī ḍaboyā . Play. Chaubolā . In Shastras learned, sense forsook, the house and all in ruin laid. 2. To spoil; destroy; ruin. 3. To give in marriage to a poor or impotent man. nām ḍubonā , v. a. To bring disgrace on one's name.

def: n. m. S. दुग्ध, Pālī. daddho ; Pr. duddh . 1. Milk. 1. Jaisā dudh vaisī budh . Prov. Your sense is as your mother's milk. 2. Hoṅtoṅ se abhī dūdh kī bū nahīṅ guī . Prov. His lips yet smack of his mother's milk. 3. Dūdh ko havvā le gayā . Wom. The goblin has flown away with the milk. 4. Dūdh phū, happā achchhā . Wom. Milk bitter, pap nice. ( Phū is opium applied to the nipples to keep off the child to be weaned from the breast). 5. Is kā dūdh phūlnā hai . Wom. Her milk is fattening. 6. Backchā rāt din meṅ ḍhāī ghaṛe dūdh pītā hai . Wom. A child drinks two and a half ghaṛās of milk (mother's milk) in the twenty-four hours. 7. Likkhe na paṛke, dūdh māreṅ kaṛhe! Prov. He can neither read nor write and he has plenty of milk to drink! 8. Dūdh meṅ kī makkhī kin ne chakkhī? Prov. Who would ever taste the fly in the milk? (Great cost and no advantage). 9. Dūdh kī sī makkhī nikāl-kar pheṅk dī . Prov. She cast me off as a fly from a bowl of milk. 10. Vahāṅ to chiṛyā kā dūdh bhī miltā hai . You can get even bird's milk there. 11. Jitnā ham ne dūdh pīyā hai, tumheṅ pānī bhī nahīṅ milegā , Prov. Saying. I have drank more milk than you will ever get water. 12. Dūdh bachā kar shādī kar do . Marry her away from her milk (blood-relations). 13. Gāë kā dūdh so māë kā dūdh . Prov. Cow's milk is as mother's milk. 2. ( Bot .) The juice or milk of plants; blastema. 3. Sect; caste. Āp kaun dūdh haiṅ? dūdh ubālnā, yā auṭānā, josh k. , v. a. To boil (milk). dūdhā bhātī , n. The ceremony of the bride and bridegroom eating a mess of boiled rice, milk, and sugar together, on the 4th day after marriage. dūdh utarnā , v. n. To flow (milk from the mother's breast). dūdh-ādhārī , n. m. One who lives on milk. dūdh baṛhānā, yā chhuṛānā , v. n. To wean (a child). dūdh-bhāī , n. m. A foster-brother ( birādar-i-rizāī ). dūdh bhar ānā , v. n. 1. To be full (udders, breasts). 2. To feel affection for. dūdh-bharī kaṭoriyāṅ , The breasts full of milk. dūdh-bahn , n. f. A foster sister. dūdh bechnā , v. n. 1. To sell milk. 2. To serve as a wet nurse. dūdh paṛnā , v. n. To form (the juice before the grain). dūdh yā dūdhī pilānā , v. a. To suckle; give suck to. dūdh-pilāī, dāī, dhāë , n. f. A wet nurse. dūdh pūt , n. m. Cattle and children. 1. Dūdh pūt qismāt se . Prov. Milk and children depend on fate. (Milk stands here for cattle or wealth). 2. Tū apne dūdh pūt ke āge pāë! Wom. A curse. dūdh pūt-vālī , n. f. A woman rich in cattle (wealth) and offspring. dūdh phaṭnā yā moṛ khānā , v. n. To turn (milk). dūdh-pītā yā god kā bachchā , n. m. A suckling; a child in arms. dūdh-pīte rupaë . Interest-bearing money; active capital. dūdh pīnā , v. n. To drink milk; to suck. Bachhṛe ne bāṅdh, gaū chūṅghe, Bachhṛā dūdh pīve saë, bahuaṛ, Tauṅh baiṭhī baiṭhī ūṅghe! Rus. Wom. Tie up the calf, it's drinking all the milk I say, My daughter-in-law! you just sit there and nod all day! dūdh jalnā , v. n. 1. To be burnt. 2 ( dūdh sūkhnā ) To be dry (a milch cow). dūdh churānā, yā chaṛhānā , v. n. To keep back its milk. Gāë dūdh chaṛhā gaī. dūdh dohnā, kāṛh'nā, yā nikālnā , v. a. To milk. dūdh dekhnā , v. a. To ascertain from the comparative thickness of the milk of a pregnant woman the sex of the unborn babe. Thick milk indicates a boy, thin milk a girl. 1. Yahāṅ baiṭhe kyā dūdh dete ho? Are you giving milk there? (Are you hatching eggs?). 2. Bak/?/ī ne dūdh dīyā, meṅgniyoṅ bharā . Prov. The milk the goat gave is full of its dung. (The benefit is made null by the conditions). dūdh sā , adj. White as milk; very white. Dūdh sī chāṅdī. dūdh kā pāṛā , n. A sucking calf or child. dūdh kā sā ubāl , n. m. A sudden fit of passion which is soon over. Dūdh kā sā ubāl āyā aur chalā gayā . Prov. saying. His rage boiled up like milk, and as suddenly went down. dūdh-kaṭṭū , E. n. m. A child not suckled by the mother for the full period owing to her pregnancy. Āge! larikvā dūdh-kaṭṭū bujhā hau, phir kuchh hau kā? Mag. Wom. I say! your child looks as though you had cut him off his milk. Are you in the family way again? dūdh ke dāṅt ; Sant. tovāḍāṭā , n. m. Milk-teeth. Abhi tumhāre dūdh ke dāṅt bhī nahīṅ ṭūṭe! Prov. You have not shed your milk-teeth yet! dūdh kī sī makkhī nikāl-kar pheṅknā , v. a. To cast off as a fly from milk. 1. Nikālā bazm se yūṅ apne ham ko tu ne jūṅ koī, Uṭhā-kar dūdh meṅ se, aë sitamgar! pheṅk de makkhī . Mārūf. O cruel tyrant! from your heart cast off as though You threw out of a bowl of milk a fly or so. 2. Ṭuṭbo na kalaī dudhvā ke daṅtvā, are birah ke kā jāne maramīyāṅ he nā? Bhoj. Song. Jantasārī. He has not shed his milk-teeth yet. What does he know of the pain of absence? dūdh kī tarah uphannā , v. n. To boil with rage (as milk boils over). dūdh-mā , n. f. Foster mother. dūdh-mūt yā gū mūt k. , Wom. v. n. To bring up an infant. dūdh-mogrī , n. Nerium coronarium ; the double white rose-bay, or oleander. Harris . dūdh-vālī , n. f. 1. A milkmaid. 2. A mother who suckles her child. dūdhoṅ nahāo, pūtoṅ phalo! A benediction. May you bathe in milk and rear numberless children! (May you abound in cattle and children!) dūdh-hāṅḍī, dudhaiṅḍī, duhāvnī , n. f. An earthen vessel for holding milk. āṭe kā dūdh , The starch of flour. chiṛyā kā dūdh, lit . Sparrow's milk. A mare's nest.
more: s. m. 1. Milk. 2. The juice (or milk) of certain bushes. دودهـ آدهاري dūdh-ādhārī ( दुग्धाहारी ) adj. Living on milk. دوده بڙهنا dūdh baṛhnā, v. n. To be weaned (a child). دودهـ يهاٴي dūdh-bhāʼī, s. m. A fosterbrother. دودهـ پلانا dūdh-pilānā, To suckle. دودهـ پلاٴي dūdh-pilāʼī, s. f. A wet nurse. دودهـ هنڐي dūdh-hanḍī, s. f. A vessel for holding milk, a milkpan.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دودھ (dvdh /⁠dūdh⁠/) (c. 1411), / دود (dvd /⁠dūd⁠/) (c. 1635), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀤𑁆𑀥 (duddha), from Sanskrit दुग्ध (dugdhá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dʰugdʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰugʰ-tó-s, from *dʰewgʰ- (“to yield”).

def: intj. S. दर्वा Mercy! justice? 1. Lūṭā mujhe butoṅ ne, duhāī Khudā kī hai! Amānat. 2. Akbar ki duhāī hai! na māne qasāī hai . 3. Duhāī Sarkār kī, duhāī sāhibān-i-angrez kī . Cry of suitors at an Indian Court of Justice. duhāī pher'nā , v. a. To make a proclamation. duhāī denā , v. a. To cry for justice, mercy, quarter or help. 1. Nālā is shor se kyoṅ merā duhāī detā, Aë falak! gar tujhe ūṅchā na sunāī detā! Zauq. Why should I cry to Heaven so loud? ah why? Except that Heaven is deaf to all my cry. 2. Kyoṅ duhāī de rakkhī hai ? Why do you make such a dreadful noise? duhāī tihāī k., yā machānā , v. a. To cry aloud for justice; to make re-iterated complaints.
s.m. A widower who marries again (cf. dojhā and dūj-bar).

def: s.f. A shop, workshop; a business
more: n. f. A shop ( hāṭ ). 1. Saudā bik gayā aur dukān rah gaī . Prov. The goods are sold, the shop remains. (Her bloom is gone, the frame remains). 2. Hot susaṅgat sahaj sukh, dukh kusaṅg ke ṭhān, Gandhī aur lūhār kī dekho baiṭh dukān . Brind satsahī. With good men you have ease, ill company's a root of pain, At the smithy's you get soot, at the perfumer's sweet scents your gain. dūkān baṛhānā yā baṅd k. , v. a. To close the shop. dūkān chalnā , v. n. To do a good business. dūkān-dār ; Pop. dukān-dār , n. m. A shopkeeper. dūkān-dārī ; Pop. dukān-dārī , n. f. Shopkeeping; trade; dealing; buying and selling. dūkān-dārī kī bāteṅ , n. f. lit . Shop men's talk. 1. Mendacity; falsehood; make-believe; humbug. Dūkān-dārī kī bāteṅ jāne do, ṭhīk mol kaho . 2. Arts; trickery; gammon. dūkāṅ sī lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To set out in order as in a shop. 2. To scatter about as in a shop. dūkān k. , v. a. To keep…
s. f. (in dakh. s. m.) A shop, a work-shop. دوکان دار dūkān-dār, A shopkeeper. دوکانداري dūkān-dār-ī, s. f. Shopkeeping.
subs: (-kī), To remove the shop or business (of); to mar or ruin the business (of)

def: n. m. S. दुःख afflict. Suffering ( pīṛā , 2); distress. 1. Ibn-i-Maryam huā kare koī, Mere dukh kī davā kare koī! Gālib. The Virgin Mary's son be he who may! Would some one only take my pain away! 2. Jāko Prabhū dārun dukh deṅhīṅ, Wākī mat pahle har leṅhīṅ . Rāmāyan. The man on whom God wills his heavy hand to lay, His guiding sense first from him he doth take away. 3. Jap, jog, virāg, mahā-makh, sādhan, dān, dayā, dam koṭī kare, Muni, sidh, suresh, Mahesh, Ganesh se sevat janm anek mare, Nigmāgam giyān Purān paṛhe, tapsa nal meṅ jug punj jare, Man se, pran rop kahe Tulsī, "ek Rām binā dukh kaun hare ?" Kabit. Though beads you tell, on His name dwell, give alms, your passions slay, Or mercy shew a million times, or sacrifice you pay, Or sage, or perfect man, Ganesh, Mahesh, you serve each day, Deep knowledge yours, versed in Purans, in fiery penance lay For ages, yet know that unblessed by Ram vain's your essay. dukh uṭhānā , v. n. 1. To bear pain; suffer; experience difficulties. 2. To take pains. dukh baṭānā , v. a. To share one's sorrows. Hameṅ kyā jo kisī kā dukh baṭāveṅ, Kisī ke vāste dunyā se jāveṅ? Jurat. What's it to me that I another's grief should share? Why for another's sake should I my life out wear? dukh bisārnā , v. n. To forget one's griefs. Kām usrā, dukh bisrā, chhāch na det ahīrā . Prov. His work all done, his grief forgot, no buttermilk from cowherd to be got. dukh bharnā, bhognā, yā bhugatnā , v. n. To labour; toil; suffer. Dukh bhareṅ bī Fā/?/khtah, aur kavve meve khāëṅ . Prov. The labour (of rearing) is my lady dove's, the fruit (brood) is devoured by the crow. (One soweth and another reapeth) dukh pānā , v. a. To suffer pain or trouble; to be afflicted. Dukh pāne-vāle laṛke jo dunyā meṅ āte haiṅ, Lachchhan sab unke pahle hī paichhāne jāte haiṅ . Nazīr. When children doomed to suffering in this world appear, Signs of their future from the first shown are quite clear. dukh-dāī , adj. Painful; grievous; troublesome; irksome; oppressive; vexatious. Sunahūṅ asantan ker subhāū! Bhūlehū saṅgatī kariye na kāū! Tin kar saṅg sadā dukh-dāī, Jimi kapilahīṅ ghāle harhāī . Rāmāyan. Now mark the ways of wicked men! Who in their company have been Have suffered grief and pain, as when Stray goats the house goat has let in. dukh dard kā sharīk , A partner in one's griefs and sorrows. dukh dard meṅ sharīk h. , v. n. To share one's griefs and sorrows; to sympathise or condole with one. dukh-dhandā , Labour; hard work; toil and trouble. dukh denā , v. a. To give pain ( taklīf denā ). dukh-sāgar , n. m. A sea of troubles ( bahr-i-gam); a world of misery. dukh sukh , Pain and pleasure. 1. Dukh sukh sab ke sāth lagā huā hai . Prov. Pain and pleasure are with us ever. 2. Dukh, sukh, nis din saṅg haiṅ, meṭ sake na koë, Jaise chhāyā deh kī nyārī nek na hoë . Dohā. Pain and pleasure with us ever, none can sever! As you wish it ne'er can be, no, parted never. dukh-kā-mārā , adj. Afflicted; wretched. dukh lagnā , v. n. To feel pain. Hoë na bishai birāg, bhavan basat bhayā chauthpan, Hirdai bahut dukh lāg, janm gayaū Harī-bhajt bin . Rāmāyan. The love of pleasure will not quit this frame, life's last stage's on, Sore is my heart, Har worship due unpaid, my life's thread spun. dukh-hartā, dukh bhanjan , n. One who relieves or removes pain or sorrow. dukh-haran , n. m. An anodyne.
more: s. m. 1. Pain, ail, ache, grief, affliction. 2. Labour, fatigue. 3. Annoyance, distress. دکهـ بهرنا dukh bharnā, To labour, to toil, to suffer distress or trouble. دکهـ پانا dukh pānā, To be afflicted. دکهـ داٴي dukh-dāʼī, or دکهـ داني dukh-dānī, adj. Giving or occasioning pain, troublesome, vexatious. دکهـ کا مارا dukh kā mārā, adj. Afflicted. دکهـ هرن दुःखहरण dukh-haraṇ, Anodyne. دکهـ هرتا दुःखहर्ता dukhahartā, Dispeller of grief or pain.

Tanū ro lūṅ, Rām! dukhī kar dī! Pūt, khasam, purkhā sab māre! Pīsan ko chākī dhar dī . Rus. Wom. song. I'm at your mourning, Ram! accursed may you be! Childless and widowed be! may all your elders die! Why set you this hard task of grinding corn 'fore me? dukhī h. , v. n. To be sorely afflicted or distressed.

more: Wom. n. f. S. दुःखी A great sufferer; an unfortunate wretch. 1. Ban, bālak, aur bhaiṅs, ukhārī, Jeṭh mā/?/ ye chār dukhārī . Prov. Trees, babies, sugarcane, and buffalo, All sufferers are when hot May winds do blow. 2. Dukhiyā dukh rove, sukhiyā kamar ṭoë . Prov. The wretched weep and wail, the well-to-do their pockets feel. (Said of lawyers). 3. Kit bhag jāuṅ? kit uṭh jīūṅ? sūṅ maiṅ be-bas dukhyārī! Rus Wom. song. Ah whither shall I fly! where go! ah helpless wretched me! 4. Mohe nīkī lāge sūrat tihārī! Tum to ho Mohan apnī garaj ke! Maiṅ birhan dukhyārī! Song. Qadar. Such pleasure 'tis for me your face to see! You, Mohan, but pursue your pleasures free! Alas poor widowed heart! ah wretched me! 2. A widow ( rāṅd ). Mujh dukhyā ko na chheṛo. janam kā dukhiyā , A wretch foredoomed to life-long suffering.
adj. In distress or pain, unfortunate, poor, indigent, afflicted.

ety: dukh˚ = Prk. दुक्ख(इ) or दुक्खे(इ) = S. दुःखय(ति), fr. दुःख
def: v.n. To pain, ache, smart, throb; to feel pity or compassion (for); to feel compunction or remorse.
more: v. n. 1. To ache; pain; throb. Abe, chhoṛ! merā pāoṅ dukhtā hai . Said by a thief. Let go my leg, fellow, it hurts. ☞ Native burglars, after they have made a hole through a wall, go in with their legs foremost, judging it better to lose a leg than the head. In this instance the legs being seized from within, the burglar, with a shrewd knowledge of human instincts, makes the prompt appeal upon which the other instinctively lets go. 2. To grudge; feel compunction or remorse; lie heavy on one's mind.
v. n. To ache, to pain, to smart.
wety: Urdu verb set دِکْھنا (dikhnā) دیکْھنا (dekhnā) دِکھانا (dikhānā) دِکھوانا (dikhvānā)Intransitive form of دیکھنا .

v.n. To live a life of toil and trouble
(apnā), To bewail (one's) woes or troubles, to tell (one's) misery, &c.
(apnā), To go about telling (one's) grievances or troubles.

def: Wom. n. m. 1. Great or constant sufferings; hard labour; a life of suffering. 1. Morī toṛ daī kāyā sārī, Pīsan kā dukhṛā bhārī . Wom. Rus. song. Hard toil it is to turn and turn the mill, My bones are broken as I grind on still. 2. Chār paise kā dukhṛā bhī nahīṅ ātā . Wom. Not even a two-penny hard job can I get. 3. Na pīse na kūṭe, na dukhṛā kare, /?/Khudā aisī ٴaurat ko gārat kare! She 'll neither grind nor pound, nor sweat for daily bread, Out on such creatures? would to God that she were dead! 4. Ham apnā dukhṛā meṅ lagal hiyā, tohrā saṅgh nijārā māre ke jāë? Mag. I am in trouble here, and you want me to flirt with you! 2. The story of one's sufferings; a tale of woe; a sad story. 1. Ghar ghar sās kā dukhṛā rotī phirti hai . Wom. From house to house she repeats the story of her sufferings at the hands of her mother-in-law. 2. Na more dīyā bātī, na more jhupṛā, Phūṅkūṅ phūs, dikhāūṅgī mukhṛā: Āj rah jā, bālam! kahūṅ dukhṛā! Rus. song. Nor lamp nor light, nor e'er a hut I own, Some straw picked up I'll fire; my face just shown. To-night, dear, stay! but hear my sad heart's moan! dukhṛā paṛnā , Wom. v. n. 1. To be afflicted. 2. To become a widow ( rāṅḍ ho jānā ). dukhṛā pīṭnā , v. n. To work or labor hard; to have a hard life of it. Din bhar dukhṛā pīṭtī hai . Wom. dukhṛā ronā , Wom. v. n. To tell the story of one's sufferings; narrate one's sorrows.

Aë Zauq! dekh! dukhtar-i-raz ko na mūṅh lagā! Chhuṭṭī nahīṅ hai mūṅh se yeh kāfir lagī huī! Zauq. O Zauq! let not the daughter of the grape thy lips touch e'er!
